header.location: Portland, OR (Open to Remote or Relocation)

summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Strategic Program Manager who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, developing processes grounded in evidence, and collaborating with cross-functional stakeholders to drive operational excellence. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can keep programs moving—through organized, data-driven approaches.

bullets.text: Own end-to-end {coord|lifecycles} for 20+ concurrent {prog|programs} with 10+ vendors—intake through launch—refining delivery and operations via {data|feedback analysis}
Coordinate across {cross|cross-functional teams} (Marketing, Sales, Ops) to launch 15+ {prog|programs}, surfacing risks early and driving open items to closure
Build {coord|SOPs}, workflows, and a {tools|digital resource library} for 70+ staff, cutting onboarding time 75% by removing the friction blocking adoption
Run needs assessments with 25+ global {cross|stakeholders} and facilitate {tools|webinar-platform} sessions for 600+ participants to confirm readiness and alignment
Facilitated organizational change by directing a Mergers & Acquisitions project, overseeing {cross|cross-functional stakeholders} and milestones
Optimized {coord|onboarding} processes across 3 global locations by conducting process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities
Initiated a DEI pilot with an Inclusive Leadership workshop for 12 senior leaders, later expanded to 23 other global locations
Strengthened engagement via an HR newsletter and award-winning well-being initiatives, recognized among 1,000+ competitors
Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming {data|feedback data} into actionable reports that informed engagement strategies
Analyzed 2,000+ employee responses, reporting insights and recommending actions to the C-suite on satisfaction drivers
Provided consultations to improve strategic plans and internal training on DEI, Inclusive Leadership, and performance review
Improved {ex|employee experience} via 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects spanning survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D
Facilitate CEO/HR discovery and stakeholder interviews to diagnose absenteeism drivers, then analyze leave {data|data} to quantify the cost
Run focus groups and 1:1 interviews with an NGO leadership team, diagnosing root causes to redesign decision-making and accountability structures
Developed proficiency across various HR functions, ranging from recruitment and compensation to performance management
Filled a challenging international role by drafting job descriptions, posting openings, and researching global compensation details
Engaged global talent via personalized communication and customer service, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
Organized recruiting events and moderated panels with C-suite at top 3 universities, reaching 230+ students and 80+ applicants
